:::{#tbl-gaussian-vs-mle-tests}

| Inference goal | Exact test (Gaussian) | Exact test statistic | Approximate test (CLT-based MLE) | Approximate test statistic |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Single coefficient | t-test | $T_{n-p}$ | Wald z-test | $Z \sim N(0,1)$ |
| Linear combination of coefficients | t-test | $T_{n-p}$ | Wald z-test | $Z \sim N(0,1)$ |
| Nested models ($q$ constraints) | Partial F-test | $F_{q,\, n-p}$ | Likelihood ratio test or Wald test | $\chi^2_q$ |
| One-sample mean | One-sample t-test | $T_{n-1}$ | z-test | $Z \sim N(0,1)$ |
| Two-sample means | Two-sample t-test | $T_{n_1+n_2-2}$ | z-test | $Z \sim N(0,1)$ |
| $K$-group means (ANOVA) | F-test | $F_{K-1,\, n-K}$ | Likelihood ratio test or Wald test | $\chi^2_{K-1}$ |

Mapping of exact Gaussian-assumption tests to their approximate CLT-based MLE equivalents.
:::

::: notes
The exact tests assume $Y_i \siid N(\mu_i, \sigma^2)$;
the approximate tests hold asymptotically for any distribution
by the central limit theorem (CLT) for MLEs.
:::
